# marmap2

`marmap2` imports, plots and analyses bathymetric and topographic data
in R. It is a modernisation workspace for the historical `marmap`
package, with tidier function names, updated documentation and newer
data import workflows.

``` r
library(marmap2)

bathy <- get_noaa(
  lon1 = -6, lon2 = -5,
  lat1 = 49, lat2 = 50,
  resolution = 1,
  class = "bathy"
)

plot(bathy, image = TRUE, land = TRUE)
```

The package also includes an importer for the official GEBCO download
service:

``` r
gebco <- get_gebco(
  lon1 = -6, lon2 = -5,
  lat1 = 49, lat2 = 50,
  resolution = 1,
  class = "bathy"
)
```

# Package index

## Data import

<!-- end list -->

  - `get_noaa()` : Download bathymetry from NOAA ETOPO 2022
  - `get_gebco()` : Download bathymetry from the GEBCO download service
  - `read_bathy()` : Read bathymetric data in XYZ format

## Bathy conversion and validation

<!-- end list -->

  - `as_bathy()` : Convert to bathymetric data in an object of class
    bathy
  - `as_xyz()` : Convert to xyz format
  - `bathy_to_tbl()` `tbl_to_bathy()` : Convert between bathy objects
    and tibbles
  - `as_sf()` : Convert bathymetric data to sf
  - `as_spatraster()` : Convert bathymetric data to a terra SpatRaster
  - `project_bathy()` : Project bathymetric grids
  - `is_bathy()` : Test whether an object is of class bathy
  - `check_bathy()` : Sort bathymetric data matrix by increasing
    latitude and longitude

## Plotting

<!-- end list -->

  - `geom_bathy()` : Plot bathymetric grids with ggplot2 and sf
    coordinates
  - `geom_coastline()` : Draw the coastline from bathymetric data
  - `coord_sf_antimeridian()` : Coordinate system for sf data around the
    antimeridian
  - `geom_sf_antimeridian()` : Plot sf bathymetry around the
    antimeridian
  - `scale_fill_bathy()` `bathy_palette()` `bathy_palettes()` :
    Bathymetry colour scales for ggplot2

## Utilities

<!-- end list -->

  - `collate_bathy()` : Collates two bathy matrices with data from
    either sides of the antimeridian

## Package

<!-- end list -->

  - `marmap2` `marmap2-package` : Import, plot and analyze bathymetric
    and topographic data

  - `summary_bathy()` :
    
    Summary of bathymetric data of class `bathy`

# Articles

### Vignettes

  - [Making and using bathymetric maps in R with
    marmap](https://besibo.github.io/marmap2/articles/marmap.md):
  - [Data import and export strategies in
    marmap](https://besibo.github.io/marmap2/articles/marmap-import-export.md):
  - [Analysing bathymetric data in R with
    marmap](https://besibo.github.io/marmap2/articles/marmap-data-analysis.md):
